Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze the Event Log I need to calculate the total resolution time for each case Total resolution time Time of Close Ticket minus Time of Receive Ticket I also need to look at individual step durations to identify bottlenecks

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive 2024 03 01 08 00 Close 2024 03 01 10 15 Total 2 hours 15 minutes Steps Receive to Triage 10m Triage to Assign 10m Assign to Investigate 40m Investigate to Resolve 60m Resolve to Close 15m Max step Investigate 60m No escalation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 2024 03 01 08 05 Close 2024 03 02 09 15 Total about 25 hours 10 minutes Steps Receive to Triage 25m Triage to Assign 30m Assign to Escalate 150m Escalate to Investigate 150m Investigate to Resolve 1350m Wait 2024 03 01 14 00 to 2024 03 02 09 00 That is 19 hours 19 times 60 equals 1140m Resolve to Close 15m Max step Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Escalation present

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive 2024 03 01 08 10 Close 2024 03 01 09 30 Total 1 hour 20 minutes Steps Receive to Triage 5m Triage to Assign 15m Assign to Investigate 15m Investigate to Resolve 30m Resolve to Close 15m Max step Triage to Assign 15m or Assign to Investigate 15m No escalation Very fast

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 2024 03 01 08 20 Close 2024 03 02 08 30 Total 24 hours 10 minutes Steps Receive to Triage 40m Triage to Assign 30m Assign to Investigate 210m equals 3 point 5 hours Investigate to Resolve 1110m equals 18 point 5 hours 2024 03 01 13 00 to 2024 03 02 08 00 That is 19 hours Resolve to Close 30m Max step Investigate to Resolve 19 hours No escalation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 2024 03 01 08 25 Close 2024 03 03 09 30 Total about 49 hours 5 minutes Steps Receive to Triage 10m Triage to Assign 25m Assign to Investigate 10m Investigate to Escalate 150m equals 2 point 5 hours Escalate to Investigate 1080m equals 18 hours 2024 03 01 14 00 to 2024 03 02 14 00 That is 24 hours Investigate to Resolve 1020m equals 17 hours 2024 03 02 14 00 to 2024 03 03 09 00 That is 19 hours Resolve to Close 30m Max step Escalate to Investigate 24 hours Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Escalation present

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compare Total Times 101 2h 15m 102 25h 10m 103 1h 20m 104 24h 10m 105 49h 05m Average about 20 point 4 hours Median 24h 10m Outliers Long cases 102 104 105 Fast cases 101 103 Conclusion Cases 102 104 and 105 have significantly longer resolution times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Escalation to Level 2 Big delay Assign to Escalate 2 point 5h Escalate to Investigate 2 point 5h Investigate to Resolve 19h The 19 hour gap between Investigation and Resolution is huge Also the investigation starts on Monday 14 00 resolution on Tuesday 09 00 This looks like an overnight gap Pattern Escalation plus Long wait after investigation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 No escalation Big delay Assign to Investigate 3 point 5h Investigate to Resolve 19h The 19 hour gap is the killer Monday 13 00 to Tuesday 08 00 Pattern Long wait after investigation no escalation This suggests the Level 1 agent is stuck or waiting for something or the investigation takes a long time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Escalation to Level 2 Big delay Investigate to Escalate 2 point 5h Escalate to Investigate 24h Investigate to Resolve 19h The Escalate to Investigate gap is 24 hours Monday 14 00 to Tuesday 14 00 The Investigate to Resolve gap is 19 hours Tuesday 14 00 to Wednesday 09 00 Pattern Escalation plus Long wait after investigation plus Long wait after escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Commonalities in Long Cases All long cases 102 104 105 have a massive delay between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket 102 19 hours 104 19 hours 105 19 hours This is a very strong signal The resolution step itself seems to take about 19 hours in delayed cases Also escalations add waiting time 102 has 2 point 5h plus 2 point 5h wait 105 has 2 point 5h plus 24h wait

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 has no escalation but still has the 19h delay This suggests the 19h delay is intrinsic to the Resolve phase for complex issues or it is a systemic issue like End of Day processing or waiting for external dependencies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at timestamps 102 Investigation 14 00 Monday to Resolve 09 00 Tuesday 104 Investigation 13 00 Monday to Resolve 08 00 Tuesday 105 Investigation 14 00 Tuesday to Resolve 09 00 Wednesday Pattern Investigation happens mid afternoon Resolution happens next morning This strongly suggests a Next Business Day cycle or an overnight batch process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Investigation 09 00 to Resolve 10 00 Same day Fast Case 103 Investigation 08 45 to Resolve 09 15 Same day Fast So simple issues are resolved same day Complex issues which might be the ones that get escalated or just take longer to investigate spill over to the next day Case 104 spilled over Case 105 spilled over twice Case 105 has an escalation wait of 24 hours That is another day lost

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root Causes Escalation Latency Escalations introduce waiting periods Case 105 waited 24 hours for Level 2 to pick up Case 102 waited 2 point 5 hours Investigation to Resolution Overnight Gap The most consistent factor is the about 19 hour gap between Investigation and Resolution in all long cases This corresponds to a Next Day resolution Cases resolved quickly happen in the morning Cases that take longer investigate in the afternoon and resolve the next morning Complexity versus Escalation Case 104 did not escalate but still took two days This implies that not all slow cases are escalated but slow cases share the Next Day pattern Perhaps the triage or assignment is not catching complexity early enough or Level 1 agents are taking too long and rolling over Wait Times 104 Assign to Investigate is 3 point 5 hours This is longer than 101 103 105 105 Escalate to Investigate is 24 hours This is a major bottleneck in the escalation process

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Reduce escalation wait times 24 hours is too long SLA needed Investigate the Next Day pattern Why do investigations in the afternoon take a full day to resolve Is it a batch job Is it manual work that gets pushed to tomorrow Improve Triage Automatic Routing Case 104 took 3 point 5 hours to assign No 40m triage 30m assign Wait Assign to Investigate is 3 point 5h Maybe assign to wrong agent Case 105 Escalate to Investigate 24h Level 2 is a bottleneck Overall Focus on reducing the time between Investigation start and Resolution The data suggests a hard constraint or process step that forces overnight wait

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Sanity Check Did I calculate Case 104 correctly Receive 08 20 Close 08 30 next day That is 24h 10m Yes Did I calculate Case 105 correctly Receive 08 25 Monday Close 09 30 Wednesday Monday 08 25 to Tuesday 08 25 is 24h Tuesday 08 25 to Wednesday 08 25 is 24h Wednesday 08 25 to 09 30 is 1h 5m Total about 49h 5m Yes Case 102 Receive 08 05 Monday Close 09 15 Tuesday Monday 08 05 to Tuesday 08 05 is 24h Tuesday 08 05 to 09 15 is 1h 10m Total 25h 10m Yes Comparison 101 and 103 are about 1 to 2 hours 102 104 105 are about 25 to 50 hours Clear separation The Investigate to Resolve gap is the smoking gun This is likely the dominant factor The recommendations must address this Also Case 105 has two overnight gaps Escalate to Investigate is Monday 14 00 to Tuesday 14 00 That is 24h It does not look like an overnight gap it looks like a full day wait Investigate to Resolve is Tuesday 14 00 to Wednesday 09 00 That is 19h So 105 has a 24h wait plus 19h wait Case 102 Escalate to Investigate is 150m 2 point 5h Investigate to Resolve is 19h Case 104 Assign to Investigate is 210m 3 point 5h Investigate to Resolve is 19h So the 19h gap is universal for slow cases The other delays vary Result The 19h gap is the primary driver Escalations add secondary delays

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Polish Cycle Times 101 2h 15m 102 25h 10m 103 1h 20m 104 24h 10m 105 49h 05m Outliers 102 104 105 Root Causes 1 Overnight Next Day Resolution Gap All slow cases show a about 19 hour delay between Investigation and Resolution suggesting tickets investigated in the afternoon roll over to the next day 2 Escalation Bottlenecks Case 105 shows a 24 hour delay waiting for Level 2 Case 102 shows moderate delay 3 Investigation Start Delays Case 104 and 102 have longer waits before investigation starts compared to fast cases Recommendations Optimize afternoon workflow to prevent rollover Reduce escalation response time SLA Analyze why Level 2 took 24 hours in Case 105 Review Triage to ensure complex tickets get faster routing Case 104 did not escalate but was slow check if Level 1 agents are overloaded
